source: Live session 2026-06-21 + Foam graph screenshot: Ash explicitly corrected his Subway order and said 'discard the prev wrong one'; Pi replied 'deleted the old one' â€” but the vault/Foam still shows the stale L2 fact 'Oregano bread, chicken, no sauce, extra veggies'.
title: L2 supersession missing: corrected facts stay active in organized_memory (stale in vault, can re-promote to L3)
current_state: Conflict resolution only invalidates L3. _invalidate_l3_entry (tools_memory.py:1628) sets invalid_at on l3_cache + l3_active_memory metadata, but does NOTHING to the corresponding L2 organized_memory row. So after a correction: (1) the old L2 fact stays status='active' forever; (2) the vault is a read-only projection of L2 (per the Subway hub note) so it keeps showing the contradicted fact; (3) promote_l2_to_l3 (tools_memory.py:1946) can re-promote that un-invalidated L2 fact back into L3 â€” resurrecting the wrong answer after L3 already invalidated it (zombie fact). Standalone L2 'note' facts (like the subway order) have no supersession path at all; only retention (archive by age/importance, :1820) and profile_structured merge (:1389) touch L2, neither of which is correction-driven.
target_state: When a new fact supersedes an old one, the supersession propagates to L2: the losing organized_memory row is marked status='archived' (or superseded, with metadata.superseded_by) in the SAME operation that invalidates L3 â€” so the vault projection drops it AND promote_l2_to_l3 can never resurrect it. SQLite is source of truth; the Supabase mirror follows. After Ash corrects a fact, the old version disappears from recall, vault, and Foam, and stays gone across sessions.
migration_plan: 1. Add _invalidate_l2_entry(entry_id, by_entry_id) mirroring _invalidate_l3_entry: set organized_memory status='archived' (+ metadata.superseded_by) in SQLite-of-record and the Supabase mirror. Use the existing replication-log append so divergence (T-174) is auditable.

2. At the conflict/supersession site, when an L3 entry is invalidated due to a contradicting new fact, also resolve the L2 row(s) that produced it (match by content/source id) and invalidate them. If the L2->L3 link isn't tracked, add a lightweight source_l2_id on promotion (promote_l2_to_l3) so the reverse lookup is exact rather than fuzzy.
3. promote_l2_to_l3: skip rows where status!='active' (never promote archived/superseded facts) â€” this closes the zombie re-promotion loop even if a link is missed.
4. Reconcile with T-232 (the invalid_at-as-column Supabase error) so L2 + L3 invalidation both use the metadata-JSONB shape the tables actually have.

5. Tests: testing/test_l2_supersession.py â€” correcting a fact archives the old L2 row (status!='active'); the archived row is excluded from vault projection + memory_read; promote_l2_to_l3 never re-promotes it; end-to-end: write fact A, write contradicting fact B, assert only B is recalled from L2 and L3 and A is gone from both.
risk_notes: This is the conversation-coherence core (the recurring write/read divergence bug). Match by content/source carefully â€” over-eager L2 archiving could wipe legitimately-distinct facts; prefer an exact L2->L3 source link over fuzzy text matching. SQLite stays source of truth; if Supabase mirror fails, local correctness must still hold. Coordinate with T-232 so both don't fight over the invalid_at representation.
